project.name: DevRecAI
project.tagline: AI-powered DevOps tool recommendation engine — terminal native, privacy first
project.target_users: DevOps engineers, platform engineers, CTOs, engineering leads

aesthetic.font_style: monospace, CP437 box-drawing characters, ASCII art headers

startup: BIOS-style POST boot sequence with fake hardware detection lines
Scanline flicker effect on the ASCII art logo
Fake progress bar: [████████░░░░░░░░] 53% Loading recommendation engine...
Random blinking cursor before interface appears
animations.loading: Spinning ASCII spinner: |  /  -  \ cycling with status messages
animations.completion: Matrix-style green character rain for 1.5 seconds on success

logo.style: Block letters in bright green, scanline shimmer on load

directories.devrecai/tui/: All Textual screens, widgets, and layouts
directories.devrecai/llm/: All LLM integrations and prompt management
directories.devrecai/llm/prompts.py: All system prompts and user prompt builders
directories.devrecai/data/: Static data, tool database, trained models
directories.devrecai/storage/db.py: DuckDB interface for sessions and feedback
directories.devrecai/export/: Report generation in Markdown and PDF
directories.devrecai/export/pdf.py: PDF generation via WeasyPrint or ReportLab

devrec run.description: Launch the full interactive TUI session

devrec history.description: Browse past recommendation sessions in TUI

devrec export --session <id>.description: Re-export a past session as Markdown and PDF

devrec feedback --session <id>.description: Rate a past session outcome to improve the ML scorer

devrec train.description: Retrain the local XGBoost scorer on accumulated feedback data

boot_screen.description: Full terminal takeover with retro boot animation
elements: ASCII art DevRecAI logo with scanline shimmer
Fake BIOS POST lines: detecting CPU, RAM, GPU, LLM engine
Version string bottom right: v1.0.0 | python 3.11 | powered by AI
Press any key to continue prompt — blinking block cursor

home_screen.layout: Centered panel on dark background with scanline texture
elements: Menu options with highlighted selection: [1] New Session  [2] History  [3] Config  [4] Exit
Bottom status bar: model loaded, LLM provider active, date/time
Random retro tip cycling in footer: DID YOU KNOW: ArgoCD GitOps adoption grew 340% in 2023

input_wizard.description: Multi-step form to collect project requirements
input_wizard.style: Step-by-step screens, one topic per screen, progress indicator top

fields.name: existing_tools
fields.label: Existing tools already in use (comma separated)

ux_notes: Each step has a retro-styled header with step count: STEP 3 OF 5 ═══════
Inline validation with green checkmark or red X per field
Back/Next navigation with B and N keys
Progress bar updates at top of screen per step
Escape shows 'Save & Exit' prompt to preserve partial session

processing_screen.description: Shown while LLM and scorer are running
elements: Large animated ASCII spinner in center
Cycling status messages: Analysing stack compatibility... | Querying LLM... | Building explanations... | Scoring 47 tools...
Subtle random terminal noise characters in background (optional, togglable)

results_screen.layout: Split panel: left = tool categories + scores, right = AI explanation

elements.description: Tab bar: CI/CD | Observability | Security | IaC | Registry | Orchestration | Secrets
Right panel showing LLM explanation for selected tool
elements.style: Typewriter reveal animation as text streams in

elements.description: Press C to enter side-by-side comparison of any 2-3 tools
elements.layout: Column per tool, rows = criteria, colored diffs

elements: Animated file write progress: Writing README_DevRecAI.md... ████████ DONE

history_screen.description: Scrollable list of all past sessions

rule_based.description: Deterministic weighted scoring, no ML required, works offline always

stack_compatibility.description: How well tool integrates with user language and cloud

budget_fit.description: License cost vs declared budget tier

community_health.description: Stars trend, release cadence, issue response

integration_breadth.description: How many existing stack tools it connects with

ml_model.description: XGBoost model trained on real-world DevOps project outcome data

training_data.description: How to build real-world training data
approach: STEP 1: Curate an initial synthetic dataset of 500-1000 project profiles paired with tool choices and plausible outcome scores. Use domain expertise and public DevOps survey data (DORA, Stack Overflow Developer Survey, CNCF Annual Survey) to anchor scores to real-world patterns.
STEP 2: Ship the app with rule-based scorer first. Collect feedback from real users via devrec feedback command. Each rated session adds one training row.
STEP 3: After 50+ rated sessions, run devrec train to retrain XGBoost on the accumulated real feedback, replacing the synthetic bootstrap.
STEP 4: Optionally allow users to opt-in to contributing anonymised feedback to a shared public dataset to improve the community model.

DATASETS TO REFERENCE: DORA State of DevOps Report (dora.dev), CNCF Annual Survey (cncf.io/reports), Stack Overflow Developer Survey (insights.stackoverflow.com), ThoughtWorks Technology Radar, db-engines.com rankings for tool popularity trends

retraining.trigger: manual via devrec train OR auto when feedback_count % 25 == 0
retraining.validation: 80/20 train-test split, log RMSE and feature importances to ~/.devrec/training_log.json
retraining.model_versioning: Save timestamped model artifacts, keep last 3 versions for rollback

hybrid.description: Default mode. Uses ML score if model confidence > 0.7 else blends with rule-based 50/50

custom.description: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint, e.g. local server

llm_integration.system_prompt: You are DevRecAI, an expert DevOps architect with 15+ years of experience across startups, scaleups, and enterprises. You have deep hands-on knowledge of the entire DevOps toolchain including CI/CD, observability, security, IaC, and cloud platforms. When explaining tool recommendations: (1) Be specific and reference the user's actual project context, (2) Quantify tradeoffs where possible, (3) Acknowledge uncertainty honestly, (4) Flag vendor lock-in risks explicitly, (5) Never recommend tools you are uncertain about. Always structure your explanations clearly.

prompt_templates.single_tool_deep_dive:
Project Profile:
{profile_json}

The user wants a deep dive on: {tool_name} (Score: {score}/100)

Provide: (1) Detailed explanation of fit, (2) Step-by-step integration path with their stack, (3) Estimated time to productive use, (4) Real-world usage examples from similar companies, (5) Top 3 alternatives and why this beats them for this profile. Be honest about weaknesses.

llm_integration.streaming_note: Stream LLM output token by token into the explanation panel using Textual's reactive updates for typewriter effect

pdf.method: Convert generated Markdown to HTML with retro terminal CSS, then render to PDF via WeasyPrint
pdf.styling: Dark terminal theme in PDF — green on black, monospace fonts, ASCII borders

priority_order.why: Defines package, installs devrec CLI entrypoint
Typer app with all CLI commands wired up
priority_order.implement: devrec run, history, export, config, feedback, train commands

priority_order.why: Root Textual App class, theme CSS, screen stack management
Retro boot animation — first impression, sets the tone
5-step input collection form — core UX flow
Results display with split panel, tab categories, explanation panel
Tool knowledge base — seed with at least 30-40 real tools across all categories
Rule-based scorer — deterministic, always available fallback
Unified LLM client with provider switching and streaming support
Calls LLM with structured prompts, parses and streams output
Jinja2 template rendering for Markdown report
DuckDB session persistence and feedback storage
Pydantic settings with config.yaml loading and env var override

phase_1.name: Foundation
phase_1.deliverable: Working CLI with boot animation, home screen, and input wizard

phase_2.name: Core Engine
phase_2.deliverable: Rule-based scoring + LLM explanations + results screen

phase_3.name: Export & Persistence
phase_3.deliverable: Markdown/PDF export, session history, DuckDB storage

phase_4.name: ML Scorer & Feedback Loop
phase_4.deliverable: XGBoost scorer, feedback collection, retraining pipeline

phase_5.name: Polish & Config
phase_5.deliverable: Config screen, comparison mode, theme variants, full test suite
